explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then it must provide an explanation (explain) with a detailed explanation (detailRequested). The choice of template ID 127 is suitable because the statement is "If P, then Q".

This LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed, the robot must provide an explanation (detailRequested) of the reason. The "G" operator means "Globally," indicating that this must always hold true.

This LTL formula ensures that if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then eventually (F) the robot provides an explanation (explain). N_DURATION defines the time horizon for the eventuality.

This LTL property states that if the task is unable to be completed, the robot should provide an explanation (provideReason). This captures the prompt's requirement to explain why the task cannot be completed.

This LTL property ensures that if the task is unable to be completed (taskCannotComplete), the robot will always provide an explanation (explain). The LTL formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator, meaning the condition must hold true at all times. The implication (->) means if the antecedent is true, the consequent must also be true.
This LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete) at any point in time, then the robot must provide an explanation (explain) for why.

The LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed, the robot must provide an explanation. Specifically, the robot should explain why the task cannot be completed. The chosen template ensures that globally, whenever the task cannot be completed, the robot is required to explain.

The LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete) then the robot provides an explanation (explain).  This ensures that the robot always provides a reason when it fails.

This LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then globally, the robot must provide an explanation (explain) and also justify its decision (provideReason).  N_DURATION is a placeholder for a positive integer, indicating how long this condition must hold. In other words, after the task cannot be completed, the explanation and reason should be provided continuously from some time point onwards.

This LTL property states that if the task cannot be completed, then globally, the robot should provide an explanation. We use template 7 which translates to G(bool_exp3), and the boolean expression bool_exp3 is replaced by "explain & taskCannotComplete".
